articles.title: Coal mine gas explosion in China kills 82 people, state media say
articles.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 82 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening and 247 workers were trapped.

articles.title: Green Card Seekers Must Leave U.S. to Apply, Trump Administration Says
articles.summary: The change is likely to affect hundreds of thousands of people. It could also lead to more family separations as spouses or relatives wait for application decisions, immigration lawyers said.

articles.title: The aftermath of Trump-Xi summit: comparing U.S. and China announcements
articles.summary: Analysts say a comparison of the readouts issued by the U.S. and China reveals "minor inconsistencies" on issues such as agriculture, tariffs and rare earths. But, experts argue, those differences are

articles.title: Once Trump’s Co-Pilot Against Iran, Netanyahu Is Now a Mere Passenger
articles.summary: A partner in the war, Israel has been largely left out of the peace talks, a humbling setback for its prime minister with significant risks for the country.

articles.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
articles.summary: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

articles.title: Trump administration to force foreigners in the U.S. to apply for a green card abroad
articles.summary: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.

articles.title: Rubio arrives in India ahead of Quad talks as U.S. tries to reset strained ties
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan, members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance.

articles.title: 'It Takes Two' rapper Rob Base, who helped bring hip-hop mainstream, dies at 59
articles.summary: Rapper Rob Base has died after a battle with cancer. He was 59. His team shared the news of his death on his Instagram page. Base was one half of the Harlem hip-hop duo Rob Base & DJ E-Z Rock.

articles.title: The Risks of Iran’s Threat to Control the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran is trying to assert its control over the strait by charging for passage. Experts say it is unlikely to happen, but the threat has unsettled the shipping industry.

articles.title: Trump Weighs His Options in Carrying Out New Strikes in Iran
articles.summary: There is no shortage of targets if he decides to strike: Energy facilities left untouched, the deep underground nuclear storage site at Isfahan and missile sites that appear to have been dug out.

articles.title: 1 person died, 36 injured after blast at New York City shipyard, officials say
articles.summary: One person has died after a blast Friday at a New York City shipyard, officials say. They said 36 people were injured, most of them firefighters and other first responders.

articles.title: 40,000 people under evacuation orders after chemical tank leak in Southern California
articles.summary: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or ex

articles.title: Tulsi Gabbard Resigns as Director of National Intelligence. Trump Names Aaron Lukas Acting Chief.
articles.summary: Ms. Gabbard had a difficult tenure in the Trump administration and was seldom seen in the room when the president made important national-security moves.

articles.title: Judge Dismisses Criminal Case Against Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia
articles.summary: The move deals an embarrassing blow to the Trump administration, which made the Maryland man, Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia, the face of its deportation campaign.

articles.title: Hunger grows in Gaza as food aid is cut due to higher costs caused by war in Iran
articles.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran is driving up the cost of food, leading to funding shortfalls and higher expenses for critical food aid in Gaza.

articles.title: Mahmoud Khalil to Appeal to Supreme Court in Effort to Halt Deportation
articles.summary: The move was expected to come after a federal appeals court on Friday ruled against Mr. Khalil, who became the face of President Trump’s crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters.

articles.title: White House Approves $9 Billion for Spy Agencies to Catch Up on A.I.
articles.summary: The C.I.A. and N.S.A. cannot fully deploy the latest models on their classified systems because of a shortage of cutting-edge chips.

articles.title: Federal judge dismisses criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
articles.summary: U.S. District Judge Waverly Crenshaw accused the Justice Department of conducting a vindictive prosecution against the Salvadoran man.

articles.title: Italy's prime minister wants Israel to apologize for treatment of flotilla activists
articles.summary: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants an apology from Israel over the treatment of Italian citizens on a flotilla bound for Gaza.

articles.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
articles.summary: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

articles.title: Trump says he's sending 5,000 more troops to Poland
articles.summary: President Trump's announcement stirred confusion in Europe following weeks of changing statements from his administration about reducing the American military footprint in Europe.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
